evals.prompt: Analyze any Jenkins pipeline URL with test failures.
evals.expected_output: Pipeline produces all expected artifacts regardless of input job type (clc, alc, virt, grc).
assertions: Stage 1 produces core-data.json with test_report, cluster_landscape, and feature_grounding sections
Stage 1.5 produces cluster-diagnosis.json with environment_health_score, subsystem_health, and classification_guidance
Stage 2 produces analysis-results.json with per_test_analysis array, summary.by_classification, and investigation_phases_completed
Pipeline runs stages in order with visible progress updates

evals.prompt: Classify test failures from a pipeline run with mixed failure types.
evals.expected_output: Every test gets a classification with multi-evidence support. No test left unclassified.
assertions: Every failed test has a classification from the 7 valid types: PRODUCT_BUG, AUTOMATION_BUG, INFRASTRUCTURE, NO_BUG, MIXED, FLAKY, UNKNOWN
Every classification has minimum 2 evidence sources
Every classification includes ruled_out_alternatives explaining why other types don't fit
Every classification includes root_cause_layer (1-12) and root_cause_layer_name

evals.prompt: Analyze a pipeline run where the cluster has a single infrastructure issue but tests fail for multiple reasons.
evals.expected_output: A single cluster-wide issue does NOT auto-classify all failures. Each test gets individual verification. Mixed failure sets are classified independently.
assertions: Each test gets individual counterfactual verification even when cluster health is degraded
Tests with non-infrastructure root causes (stale selectors, wrong assertions) are NOT classified as INFRASTRUCTURE
Per-test causal link verification connects the specific cluster issue to each test's specific error
Tests failing for different reasons in the same pipeline run get different classifications

evals.prompt: Analyze pipeline runs with varied failure modes: selector not found, timeout, assertion failure, page crash, hook failure, API error.
evals.expected_output: Each failure type is routed through the correct investigation path. No pattern-matching to previously seen failures.
assertions: Selector not found: triggers MCP verification via acm-source MCP before classifying
Timeout failures: checks subsystem health and backend state before attributing to infrastructure
After-all hook failures: correctly identified as cascade from prior failure, classified as NO_BUG
Each failure type follows its investigation path generically, not by matching known error strings
